description:
Execute code in an isolated sandbox (microVM or WASM fallback).

EPHEMERAL (no workspace): fast, stateless, destroyed after each call.
WORKSPACE (workspace set): persistent VM — filesystem and processes survive between calls. Use the same workspace ID across calls to build a project. If the code starts a server on port 3000 the response includes a 'url' field with the exposed address.

Languages: 'javascript' (full Node.js, npm available in workspace), 'python', 'bash'.
For servers, listen on port 3000.

code.description: Code to execute. JS: use console.log(). Servers: listen on port 3000.

language.description: Runtime to use. All three are available in a workspace; ephemeral mode supports 'javascript' only.

workspace.description: Persistent workspace ID. Omit for ephemeral one-shot execution. Use the same ID across calls to share filesystem state. Destroyed when the session ends.
